text:
Amana (アマナ, Amana) was a player of the game Greed Island. She was also a member of Team Asta and a member of Kazsule's Alliance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 154 

Appearance

Amana wore a long sleeveless dress and had dark shoulder-length hair. She had a long fringe parted over her right eye and covering her left eye. In the 1999 anime adaptation, her dress is pink (purple in a flashback) and her hair is brown; in the 2011 anime adaptation, her dress is sky-blue, and her hair is dark-blue. 

Personality

Amana was quiet as well as observant and quick at deducing conditions to acquire certain cards within the game. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 155 

Background

Amana and her other teammates were fairly advanced in the game, as they collected 71 specified slot cards by the time they decided to cooperate with Gon and Killua. 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Amana, a member of Team Asta, attends a meeting of fifteen players arranged by Kazsule in order to stop the Bombers from clearing the game. As the meeting escalates, Asta gets into a heated argument with a member of Gon's team, Killua. Amana and her team member Manheim stop the argument by offering two S-rank cards in exchange for information on the Bomber's abilities. When it's decided that the newly-formed alliance will look for "Plot of Beach", Amana uses a "Guidepost" card to learn that it's in Soufrabi. The group then uses an "Accompany" card to travel there. 

After arriving at Soufrabi, Kazsule instructs them on how to acquire an SS-rank card, and the alliance members split up into small teams to go around the town to acquire information on the "Plot of Beach" card. A female NPC informs them that Razor and his fourteen devils, a group of pirates, rule over the city. She promises to tell them where the card is if they drive the pirates out of the town. Amana deduces that the NPC is only giving this information now because of certain conditions that were met (fifteen players traveling to the town with an "Accompany" card). The alliance members then make their way to the pirates' base in the town and are challenged by the pirate Bopobo to a sumo match. Killua manages to beat him, and the group is led to the pirates' lighthouse base, where they are challenged to a series of sports trials, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 156 but they fail them all. Seeing that there's no possible way for the Bombers to collect the "Plot of Beach" card, Asta convinces most of the others to leave the alliance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 157 

Amana's name is seen in Gon's binder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 After Gon and his group successfully defeat Razor and collect the rare card, they are contacted by Genthru, who mentions that Amana and the rest of the alliance members are dead. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 17, Chapter 169 

Abilities & Powers

Gon's team estimated her to be too weak to defeat Razor's convicts, as turned out to be the case. Amana was also unable to see the pirate boxer's teleporting uppercut. 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Amana was capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121
